description: This chart presents room-temperature Charpy V-notch impact energy (left y-axis, unit J/cm², black circular data points) and Brinell Hardness Number (right y-axis, grey square data points) as a function of phosphorus content (x-axis, in wt.%) for age-hardened Fe-30Mn-9Al-1Si-0.9C lightweight austenitic steels, with samples aged at 530 °C for 10 h. Error bars indicate the variability of measured values for each composition. As phosphorus content increases from ~0 wt.% to ~0.045 wt.%, impact energy drops drastically from ~137 J/cm² to ~24 J/cm², while Brinell hardness increases from ~292 to ~342. This figure supports the conclusion that phosphorus significantly enhances age hardening but causes severe embrittlement in this high Mn-Al steel system, so strict control of phosphorus content is required to retain acceptable notch toughness for application.
